name: Guard Narrowing
description: If-statement with return/throw in the consequence and no else. Code after the if implicitly assumes the condition's negation holds, but this may not be checked.
smt2Template:
(declare-const guard_condition Int)
(declare-const guard_returns Int)
(declare-const code_after_reached Int)
(assert (=> (= guard_condition 1) (= guard_returns 1)))
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 0) (= code_after_reached 1)))
(assert (= guard_condition 0))
(assert (= code_after_reached 1))
(check-sat)

teachingExample.explanation: if (!token) return 401 — code after assumes token exists but doesn't re-check
teachingExample.smt2:
(declare-const token_null Int)
(declare-const guard_fires Int)
(declare-const code_reached Int)
(assert (=> (= token_null 1) (= guard_fires 1)))
(assert (= token_null 0))
(assert (= code_reached 1))
(check-sat)
